what_it_does: Defines a single radial-view BINDING dict named 'raw' whose sectors are the distinct `kind` values present in the store, with radius from time and order by count, plus operator-facing meta {name, is, fills, why}.
observations: Described as 'THE DATA-DRIVEN DEFAULT — no hardcode' since sectors are whatever kinds exist in the store.
meta describes each dot as one event, slice=kind, distance-from-centre=how long ago (centre=now).
Comments mark the meta as a TENTATIVE SEED field-set (name/is/fills/why) pending Tim/DNA ratification, journey-gated by OPERATOR-SURFACE-LOOP.md OQ1-4.
Notes the operator never sees machine names; references composition legibility shape ch-2mnxl9j0.
standouts: Self-describes as the data-driven default view with no hardcoded sectors.
conventions: Same meta field-set (name/is/fills/why) as bindings/by_lens.py and the broader SEED legibility shape.
Operator-law: never expose machine names; plain-language meaning lives in the binding.
notes: The default live-activity radial view: sectors are the kinds present in the store, distance is recency.
questions: Where is the time->radius mapping (centre=now) actually computed and rendered?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — states it is the data-driven default kinds-by-time view and provides exactly that.
intention_signals: Trying to give the operator an at-a-glance live map of activity where the categories emerge from the data itself rather than being predeclared.
contribution: Contributes the default radial binding mapping store `kind` to sectors and time to radius, plus the operator-facing description of the live-activity view.
summary_for_embedding: A radial-view binding 'raw' whose sectors are the distinct kinds in the store and whose radius is recency, with operator-facing meaning copy.
intention_for_embedding: To give an operator an at-a-glance, data-driven live map of what kinds of things are happening and how recently.
